cap_space_post_fa: TBD — significant cap committed to Jones/Pierce deals; cap figures still settling

key_departures.impact: Traded to Steelers along with a swap of late-round draft picks (received 6th, sent 7th). Freed ~$24M in cap space. Was WR1 on the roster but carrying a massive cap number. Departure creates a WR depth need opposite Alec Pierce.
Signed Raiders to 3yr/$48M. Had a disappointing 2025 (4.0 sacks in 17 games) in a contract year. Despite the decline, was still a starter and his exit thins the EDGE room further.
Signed Falcons (terms undisclosed). Missed all of 2024 with a torn Achilles; returned in 2025 for 14 games with just 2 sacks. Departure continues the EDGE room overhaul.
Traded to Packers for DT Colby Wooden. Was the team's starting MLB; had declined in 2025 (PFF grade 38.4 vs. All-Pro 2024). Saved ~$5M in cap space net of Wooden's salary. Creates a need at LB.
Signed 2yr/$14M with Commanders. Was a starting safety for the Colts. Young, ascending player the team could not afford after committing to Jones and Pierce. Creates a need at S.

Signed Texans to 2yr/$25M ($13.5M guaranteed). Was the Colts' starting RT for 8 seasons (105 starts in 107 games). Colts appeared comfortable moving on, with 2025 4th-round pick Jalen Travis having played well in 4 late-season starts. Departure creates a starting RT competition.
Signed Seahawks (terms not disclosed). Was a depth/rotational safety. Loss is minor given he was not a starter, but compounds the S need.

Signed Bears to a 2-year deal. Was a rotational IDL who had career highs in 2025 (3.5 sacks, 38 tackles). The Colts have added multiple IDL replacements (Wooden, Clemons, Nnadi) so this is a manageable loss.

signings.analysis: Largest two-year contract in NFL history ($88M, up to $100M with incentives). $50M fully guaranteed at signing, $60M injury-guaranteed. Re-sign locks in QB who went 8-2 in 2025 before a torn Achilles ended his season. Health is the wildcard but the Colts were always re-signing him — the only question was structure. At $44M AAV, this is mid-tier starter money. QB is no longer a need, though the backup situation (Riley Leonard? Anthony Richardson?) remains unresolved.

Blockbuster 4yr/$114M re-sign with $80M guaranteed ($60M fully guaranteed at signing). Makes Pierce the highest-paid free-agent receiver in NFL history at $28.5M AAV. He led the NFL in yards per catch (21.3) for the second straight year and surpassed 1,000 receiving yards in 2025. However, the Colts simultaneously traded WR1 Michael Pittman Jr. to the Steelers, meaning Pierce now IS the WR1 and the team lacks a credible WR2. Josh Downs is the next returning receiver but the WR room has a meaningful depth hole post-Pittman trade. WR is partially addressed at the top, but a WR2 need remains.

2yr/$20M (max) with only $11M guaranteed — effectively a 2yr/$11M deal with incentives. Key is an 8-year journeyman with career-high 6.5 sacks; had 4 sacks in 12 games in 2025. Mid-tier pass rusher at best. Per PFF, ranked 70th of 115 qualifying edge defenders in 2025. Described by multiple outlets as a 'bridge/rotational' player, not a starter-caliber solution. EDGE remains a significant need — this does not address the loss of both Paye and Ebukam in any meaningful long-term sense.

3yr/$17.5M (up to $18.5M) but contract is effectively structured as a 1-year deal for the Colts — the only hard money is ~$3M prorated signing bonus and ~$2.99M base salary in Year 1. Years 2 and 3 are team options with minimal guarantees. Had career-high 4.5 sacks in 2024 but only 1 sack in 2025 with Jets. 8.5 career sacks in 4 seasons. Pure rotational piece at best. Alongside Key, provides EDGE volume but neither player is a starter-quality signing. EDGE remains a top draft priority.

signings.previous_team: Green Bay Packers (via trade for LB Zaire Franklin)
signings.analysis: Acquired via trade (for Zaire Franklin) on the final year of his rookie contract. A run-stuffer IDL who started 16 of 17 games for Green Bay in 2025 (50 tackles, 6 TFLs). Young (24) with upside as a run defender. PFF grade 50.6 in 2025. Cheap — $1.145M in 2026 — and becomes a UFA after this season. Provides IDL depth behind Buckner and Stewart. Net cap savings of ~$5M from the Franklin trade. Partially replaces Gallimore's depth role at IDL.

Contract terms not publicly disclosed at time of research. 3x Super Bowl champion with Chiefs; 130 career games, 98 starts. Primarily a run-stopping nose tackle. Had 16 tackles in 15 games in 2025 (PFF grade 43.6, 118th of 134 IDL). Pure veteran depth behind Stewart and Buckner. Will not change the IDL need picture but adds cheap, experienced depth.

Re-signed as ERFA at minimum salary. Went 11/11 on FGs and 10/10 on PATs in 5 games in 2025, including a franchise-record 60-yard FG. Will compete with Spencer Shrader (recovering from ACL/MCL) for the kicking job. No impact on draft needs.
Re-signed as ERFA at minimum salary (signed Jan 5, 2026). Had 32 tackles and 2 QB hits in 16 games in 2025. Pure depth/special teams player. Does not address the hole left by trading Zaire Franklin.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Daniel Jones re-signed to 2yr/$88M ($60M guaranteed). Largest two-year deal in NFL history locks in the starter for 2026-27. Achilles recovery is a risk but the team is fully committed. QB is no longer a draft priority. Backup situation (Leonard/Richardson?) is a modest secondary concern but does not require a premium pick.

Net neutral to slightly worse. Alec Pierce retained on elite 4yr/$114M deal (fully addressed as WR1), but Michael Pittman Jr. was simultaneously traded to Pittsburgh. Pierce is now unquestioned WR1 but the WR2 slot behind Josh Downs is genuinely open. The Colts need a complement opposite Pierce — this remains a Day 2 draft need.

Paye (Raiders) and Ebukam (Falcons) both departed. Replacements are Arden Key (2yr/$20M, rotational) and Micheal Clemons (3yr/$17.5M, effectively 1-year deal, rotational). Neither signing indicates a starter-level investment. EDGE remains a critical need — Laiatu Latu is the only reliable piece. The Colts have no first-round pick, so premium EDGE via Day 2 (2nd or 3rd round) is likely. Still the team's most urgent defensive need.

Gallimore departed but was replaced by three IDL additions: Colby Wooden (trade), Micheal Clemons (also functions as IDL), and Derrick Nnadi. DeForest Buckner and Grover Stewart remain entrenched starters. IDL depth is now adequate. Still a late-round draft pick of interest, but no longer a pressing need.

Zaire Franklin traded to Green Bay. Franklin was the starter and a two-time Pro Bowler (though 2025 was a down year). Only Austin Ajiake (depth) returned at LB. The room is critically thin. Colts will need to add a starter-quality LB via the draft (likely rounds 2-4) or potentially additional free agency moves.

Nick Cross departed (Commanders, 2yr/$14M) and Rodney Thomas II departed (Seahawks). Camryn Bynum (2025 free agent signing, 4yr/$60M) remains and is the starter. Hunter Wohler (missed 2025 with Lisfranc) is the depth option. With Cross gone, there is a starter-level hole at the second safety spot. Remains a mid-round draft need.

Braden Smith (105 career starts) signed with the Texans. Colts are internally penciling in 2025 4th-round pick Jalen Travis as the replacement RT — he played well in 4 starts (74.2 PFF grade, 1 sack allowed in 165 snaps). But Travis is still an unproven 2nd-year player. Bernhard Raimann holds down LT. The OT situation is manageable but a swing at an experienced tackle in the mid-rounds would be prudent. Elevated from depth need to genuine starter competition.

key_additions.headline: Historic re-sign (largest 2-year deal in NFL history) locks in franchise QB as he recovers from torn Achilles
Two-time NFL yards-per-catch leader retained on record-setting deal as new WR1 following Pittman trade
Journeyman pass rusher adds EDGE rotation depth to replace departed Paye and Ebukam
Rotational edge rusher continues Colts' overhaul of the defensive front on a prove-it structure
Young run-stopping IDL acquired in Franklin trade; cheap depth behind Buckner and Stewart
3x Super Bowl champion adds veteran interior depth to support Buckner and Stewart

key_losses.headline: Longtime WR1 traded to Steelers (Colts received 6th-round pick), freeing $24M cap but opening WR2 void
2021 first-round pick departs after disappointing contract year (4 sacks), leaving EDGE room critically thin
Veteran pass rusher (9.5 sacks in 2023) departs after Achilles-limited 2025 (2 sacks), completing EDGE room overhaul
2024 All-Pro tackler traded for DT Colby Wooden as Colts reset linebacker room
Starting safety (24) departs as Colts prioritized Jones/Pierce cap spending, leaving hole at S2
8-year Colts cornerstone (105 starts) signs division rival, opening RT competition for 2nd-year Jalen Travis
